stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (reported Apr 21): consolidated net profit Rs 656 cr (+19.2% YoY); NII Rs 813 cr (+10.8% YoY). FY26 PAT Rs 2,291 cr (+18% YoY). NIM up 6 bps QoQ to 3.69%; GNPA improved to 0.93%. Affordable book Rs 8,150 cr (+~61% YoY). Stock surged 11% post-results; trading near Rs 1,042 (May 5). ICICI Securities upgraded to BUY with target raised to Rs 1,350 (from Rs 990); MOFSL also Buy. Final dividend Rs 8/share proposed.
stocks.sector_context: RBI rate-easing cycle aiding HFC/NBFC NIMs — analysts expect 20-80 bps NIM expansion as high-cost borrowings refinance lower. Bank lending to NBFCs grew 19.1% in Feb 2026 (vs 6.4% prior year). Home-loan AUM expected to grow 12-13%; LAP 20%+. Ind-Ra cautions cost of funds may rise marginally medium-term amid macro uncertainty. Larger retail-focused HFCs better positioned vs smaller players facing bank competition.
stocks.peer_comparison: Best-in-cohort asset quality among mid-tier HFCs (GNPA 0.93%, below 1%). Affordable segment growth of +61% YoY materially above peer averages. Q4 disbursement momentum and NIM expansion ahead of sector. Capital adequacy comfortably above peer median. Retail-led mix preferred over corporate-heavy HFCs in current cycle.
red_flags: Trendlyne flagged ASM/GSM history as of Jan 23, 2026 — current status unverified for May 2026; merits a check before sizing up
Promoter (PNB) encumbrance disclosure filed Mar 11, 2026 — concentrated promoter exposure to a PSU bank
Ind-Ra warning that HFC cost of funds could rise marginally medium-term
catalysts: Rs 8/share final dividend pending shareholder approval
ICICI Securities target Rs 1,350 vs current ~Rs 1,042 implies ~30% upside
Continued RBI rate-easing path supporting NIM expansion into FY27
Affordable housing segment scaling toward becoming a meaningful share of book

stocks.recent_news: Trading at Rs 122-123 zone in late Apr / early May 2026 (Apr 30 close Rs 123.43). 52-week range Rs 76.36-148.14. AUM Rs 54,127 cr; expense ratio 0.80%. ETF tracks domestic gold price — no entity-specific events. Gold spot fell to $4,540/oz on May 4 (lowest since late March) amid Middle East tension reset and dollar firming.
stocks.sector_context: Q1 2026 was a record quarter for Indian gold ETFs (net 20t inflow); India contributed 32% of global ETF demand (#2 after China). AUM for gold ETFs +191% YoY to ~Rs 1.7 lakh cr. Gold + silver ETFs combined accounted for 55% of total Indian ETF inflows, surpassing equity ETFs. 2026 forecasts bullish: Goldman Sachs $5,400 EOY, JPM $6,300, RBC $5,723, Wells Fargo $6,100-6,300. Central banks bought 244t (+3% YoY) in Q1.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest gold ETF in India by AUM (Rs 54,127 cr). Tracking error and 0.80% expense ratio in line with peers; performance mirrors physical gold so no company-specific alpha. Liquidity advantage vs smaller peers given AUM scale.
red_flags: Gold spot pulled back to $4,540/oz (lowest since late March) — near-term consolidation risk
Trading ~17% below 52-week high (Rs 148.14)
Expense ratio 0.80% on the higher side for a passive instrument
January loaded most of Q1 inflows (~80%) — recent flow momentum may have softened
catalysts: US Fed rate-cut path through 2026 (markets repricing multiple cuts)
Continued central bank buying and EM safe-haven demand
INR depreciation (rupee at record low) magnifies gold returns for domestic holders
Structural shift in India toward financial gold over jewellery

stocks.recent_news: Hit all-time high Rs 1,748.80 on May 4, 2026 (+6%). Q4 FY26: revenue Rs 10,737.58 cr (+26.5% YoY); PAT Rs 3,308.30 cr (+9.4% YoY). FY26 revenue Rs 38,736 cr (+24.6% YoY); first Indian operator to cross 500 MMT cargo. April cargo 43.1 MMT (+15% YoY); container/dry cargo +17% YoY. Rail logistics -16% YoY at 48,490 TEUs. Board approved Rs 7.50/share dividend; record date June 12; AGM June 24, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: JPMorgan Overweight on Adani Ports and JSW Infra; sector cargo expected to grow 5-6.5% CAGR on long-term GDP of 6.5-7.5%. Logistics integration is the dominant strategic theme; both APSEZ and JSW Infra expanding hinterland and integrated logistics assets. JSW pursuing Rs 39,000 cr capex targeting 400 MTPA by FY30. India defense exports and trade flows supporting volume growth.
stocks.peer_comparison: India's largest private port operator and only Indian to cross 500 MMT. April cargo growth +15% YoY ~2.5x sector CAGR. Trades at P/E ~30.6x vs JSW Infra ~35.4x (cheaper); ROE 16.39% vs JSW 15.08% and Gujarat Pipavav 14.50%. Higher leverage (D/E 0.68 vs JSW 0.29). Materially ahead on scale and integrated logistics footprint.
red_flags: Promoter group sold 2% stake (Rs 7,486 cr) to Capital Group; promoter holding dropped to 66.03% from 68.02%
Rail logistics volume -16% YoY in April — segment-level softness in higher-margin business
Q4 margins contracted despite revenue surge — interest burden and margin pressure flagged by analysts
Stock at all-time high after multi-quarter rally; valuation full
Adani group historical governance overhang persists as a structural risk factor
catalysts: AGM June 24, 2026; Rs 7.50/share dividend record date June 12
23+ analysts on Strong Buy; targets Rs 1,833-1,980 (Jefferies Rs 1,980, HSBC Rs 1,950, MOFSL Rs 1,900)
May business update (May volumes) likely mid-June
Capital Group stake purchase signals institutional validation of rerating

stocks.recent_news: Share price ~Rs 4,655 on May 6, 2026; +31% since early April. Kota Ravi assumed charge as 22nd CMD on May 1, 2026 (succeeding D K Sunil, superannuated Apr 30). Pawan Hans contract for 10 Dhruv NG helicopters worth Rs 1,800 cr (delivery by 2027). Order book Rs 1.89-2.54 lakh cr (heading higher with pending Mk-1A orders). Board meeting May 14, 2026 to consider audited Q4/FY26 results. GE delivered 6th F404 engine; 20 more between June-Dec 2026; HAL targeting 24 engines in FY27. HAL had penalized GE for prior delays.
stocks.sector_context: Indian defense in multi-decade upcycle. Combined order book of top 5 defense firms ~Rs 15 lakh cr. India defense exports Rs 38,424 cr in FY26 (+62.66% YoY). DAC approved projects worth Rs 2.38 trillion in March 2026. Govt targets Rs 3 trillion defense production and Rs 50,000 cr exports by FY29. Aircraft budgetary support up 12% YoY in 2026. DAM Capital top picks: HAL, BEL, BDL, Solar Industries.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest order book among defense PSUs at Rs 1.89-2.54 lakh cr (vs BEL Rs 73,400 cr, Mazagon Dock Rs 27,415 cr, BDL Rs 25,962 cr). Order book ~3x BEL's. Outperforming defense peer set on momentum (+31% in five weeks). Tejas Mk-1A platform unique to HAL — no domestic substitute.
red_flags: Stock +31% in five weeks — sharp run-up raises near-term profit-booking risk
P/E elevated above 30x post-rally; limited margin for earnings disappointment
CMD transition (Kota Ravi from May 1) — short-term execution-continuity risk
GE F404 engine supply remains the binding constraint on Mk1A deliveries (~20 airframes awaiting engines); West Asia tensions and prior delays raise schedule risk
PSU defense valuations broadly stretched — sector-wide derating risk if budget cycle disappoints
catalysts: Board meeting May 14, 2026 — Q4/FY26 audited results (major near-term catalyst, ~8 days away)
Pawan Hans Rs 1,800 cr Dhruv NG contract booking
Pending ~97 additional Tejas Mk-1A orders expected (would lift backlog to ~180 units)
GE engine ramp from H2 2026 should unblock revenue conversion of Mk-1A backlog
Continued defense export push to Rs 50,000 cr by FY29

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (reported Apr 30): PAT Rs 213 cr (+114% YoY) vs estimate Rs 156 cr; revenue Rs 938 cr (+33.8% YoY) vs estimate Rs 807 cr; EBITDA Rs 322 cr vs estimate Rs 254 cr (+80% YoY); margin 34.2%. FY26: revenue Rs 3,314 cr (+41% YoY); EBITDA Rs 1,082 cr (margin 32.6%); PAT Rs 664 cr vs Rs 289 cr prior year. Final dividend Rs 8.6/share (430% of face value). Stock intraday high Rs 7,208.5 on results day; closed near Rs 6,793. Capex range raised to Rs 700-1,000 cr from Rs 500-600 cr.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals tailwind from China supply rationalization (export tax rebate removal effective April 2026) plus geopolitical-driven price volatility benefitting fluorochemicals. Sector projected $40 bn by 2026 at 9-12% CAGR. SRF, Aarti, Deepak Nitrite, PI Industries, Gujarat Fluorochemicals operating in same backdrop. SRF trading at P/E 42.5x suggests premium multiples broadly accepted.
stocks.peer_comparison: Outperforming peers materially: Q4 PAT +114% and FY revenue +41% vs sector average 9-12%. CDMO segment +61% YoY (industry-leading) to Rs 186 cr. New collaborations with Honeywell and Chemours (USD 14M starting, scaling 10x in liquid cooling fluids) provide differentiated growth. FY26-28E projected sales/EBITDA/EPS CAGR 23%/21%/26%.
red_flags: Premium valuation (~Rs 6,793, market cap ~Rs 35,000 cr) — limited margin for execution disappointment
CDMO concentration creates client/molecule concentration risk
Refrigerant gas pricing cyclicality and feedstock/crude exposure
Capex raised significantly — execution risk on Rs 700-1,000 cr deployment
Note: sources report Q4 PAT YoY growth as either +114% or +124% depending on base; both reflect a material beat but exact figure varies
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 8.6/share
CDMO ramp to USD 100M by FY28E (Emkay), with potential to double by FY30
Chemours partnership in liquid cooling fluids — 10x scale-up potential
Honeywell collaboration adding to CDMO pipeline diversity
China export tax rebate removal (April 2026) sustaining margin tailwind for Indian fluorochemicals
Multiple analyst Buy calls with up to 24% upside targets
